XOnlineCompetitionCancel

Cancels a tournament.

HRESULT XOnlineCompetitionCancel(
    DWORD dwUserIndex,
    DWORD dwTemplate,
    ULONGLONG qwTeamID,
    ULONGLONG qwCompetitionID,
    HANDLE hWorkEvent,
    PXONLINETASK_HANDLE *phTask
);

Parameters

dwUserIndex
[in] Controller index (zero-based) of the player. The player must have been successfully logged on with a previous call to XOnlineLogon.
dwTemplate
[in] ID of a template describing the competition.
qwTeamID
[in] Team ID. The value in this parameter is used only if this is a team competition rather than a competition between individuals. If this is not a team competition, set this value to zero.
qwCompetitionID
[in] Unique ID that specifies the competition to be canceled.
hWorkEvent
[in, optional] Handle to an event you want to associate with this task. If you do not want to assign an event, set this parameter to NULL.
phTask
[out] Pointer to an XONLINETASK_HANDLE type. The handle is associated with the task started by XOnlineCompetitionCancel.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, an error code otherwise. The following table lists some of the more common error codes returned by this function.

Error Code Description
XONLINE_E_QUERY_PERMISSION_DENIED The user or team specified is not the user or team that created the competition.
XONLINE_E_QUERY_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND The specified competition does not exist.

Remarks

Only the player or team that created the tournament can cancel it. The tournament continues to appear in users' lists of tournaments in its canceled state for a further 48 hours. Developers should determine whether or not to enforce the cancellation of tournaments that are in progress.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

XOnlineCompetitionCreate, XOnlineCompetitionCreateSingleElimination
